rubric.evidence: Standalone runnability probe (transformers.js 3.7.5, audio-classification, dtype q8, onnx-community/wav2vec2-base-Speech_Emotion_Recognition-ONNX) on a real 16 kHz speech clip returned a full 6-class distribution: DISGUST 0.643, HAPPY 0.142, ANGRY 0.115, NEUTRAL 0.036, FEAR 0.033, SAD 0.031 — real probabilities that sum to 1. Headless-Chrome full flow: first visit shows loader state=download-required + Download button (no auto-download); the page decodes the sample and runs the pipeline in a Web Worker. Screens: reports/responsive/screens/speech-emotion-recognition/desktop.png, mobile.png.
rubric.notes: Same worker/pipeline architecture as the built, verified ast-audio-classification demo.

rubric.evidence: audio-classification output is ranked emotion labels with probabilities. The page renders the full 6-class distribution as confidence bars (not just the winner), a big top-emotion readout, and a raw probability table — matching the per-task template 'produces ranked audio class labels with probabilities'. Probe output above confirms shape + semantic sanity.

At-a-glance declares the exact model id (linked), task (audio-classification), architecture (Wav2Vec2ForSequenceClassification), dtype (q8), and backend (WASM; WebGPU when an adapter exists). Inference runs off the main thread in models/speech-emotion-recognition/worker.js via the shared lib/webai.js loadPipeline.

Uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (transformers.js owns transformers-cache; lib/model-cache.js scans it). First-visit download-required + Download button confirmed. Reload auto-init uses the identical shared code path proven end-to-end on the sibling silero-vad build (reload → state=ready, no Download button).
rubric.notes: auto-init-on-cached is the shared loader path; manual-evidenced full 95 MB reload verdict pending an agent read.

rubric.evidence: Loader announces checking → downloading (progress) → ready via role=status aria-live; errors surface on the page (.status.err) with a Retry path; WASM path needs no WebGPU. The model degrades honestly (no blank/faked output). An explicit honesty caveat about acted-emotion training is shown in At-a-glance.

Controls exercised across the ladder: sample chips (aria-pressed), file dropzone (role=button, Enter/Space), mic record, Read-emotion button; practical adds a queue board; wild adds window/hop range sliders; multi-model runs two models. Keyboard/focus verdict on both classes pending an agent matrix read.
rubric.notes: auto-checkable parts pass; keyboard/tap verdict is manual-evidenced.

rubric.evidence: Every emotion bar, big readout, timeline cell, and probability row is derived from the real pipeline output — no canned distribution. The honesty caveat frames the top label as a signal, not a verdict.

Buttons have accessible names; emoji are aria-hidden with a text label alongside; status is a live region; range sliders (wild) are label-associated with visible <output>. Conformance images-have-alt / buttons-have-names pass. Full SR pass on both classes pending an agent read.

Responsive matrix (automated): desktop 1280×800 and mobile 360×740 DPR3 — no horizontal overflow, no clipped controls on either class. The emotion bars use a min-inline-size:0 track grid; the multi-model layout is a min(100%,260px) auto-fit two-column that collapses to one on mobile (css-layout guidance). Screens: reports/responsive/screens/speech-emotion-recognition/{desktop,mobile}.png.
rubric.notes: auto-checkable overflow/clipping pass; legibility/tap verdict is manual-evidenced.

rubric.evidence: Inference is off the main thread (Web Worker); the emotion-over-time (wild) page classifies windows sequentially in the worker and streams per-window results back, so the UI stays responsive (identify-inp-causes guidance). No blocking main-thread inference loop.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use auto-fit/minmax grids and flex-wrap with min-inline-size:0 rather than fixed widths so the confidence bars and the two-column multi-model layout never overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — emotion bars use a minmax track with min-inline-size:0 on the bar track; the multi-model 'words vs tone' view is a repeat(auto-fit,minmax(min(100%,260px),1fr)) grid that collapses to one column on mobile. Responsive matrix confirms no overflow at 360px or 1280px.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep interactions responsive by keeping heavy JavaScript off the interaction path.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the wav2vec2 pipeline runs in a Web Worker; the emotion-over-time page classifies windows in the worker and streams results, so the main thread only draws.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state in a live region; keep emoji decorative and expose a text label; associate sliders with visible values.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — status is role=status aria-live; emoji are aria-hidden beside a text emotion label; the window/hop sliders (wild) are label-associated with a live <output>.

openQuestions: Does the ~95 MB q8 model download + init stay within acceptable memory/time on a mid-range mobile device?
Are the emotion labels (trained on acted speech) intuitive enough that the honesty caveat is sufficient, or should confidence gating be added to the top-label readout?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for speech-emotion-recognition: Read the mobile+desktop screenshots, confirm the confidence bars + multi-model two-column layout + tap targets + focus, then flip support to ok (or unsupported+evidence).
Evaluate a smaller/quantised emotion model variant for constrained mobile (the base wav2vec2 q8 is ~95 MB).

summary: Revision-1 critique for speech-emotion-recognition (audio-classification, wav2vec2, q8). Real in-browser inference is confirmed by a standalone probe (real 6-class distribution) and the shared worker/loader architecture proven end-to-end on sibling builds. Auto-checkable dimensions pass; the full 95 MB mobile reload + tap/focus verdicts await an agent matrix read.
